recap: For x to the power (m/n): apply nth root first, then raise to m. For negative indices: take the reciprocal. Estimate roots by finding the two nearest perfect squares/cubes.

steps: 64 to the power (1/2) = square root of 64 = 8.

Q2.question: Estimate the value of the square root of 70, stating which two integers it lies between.

steps: 8 squared = 64, 9 squared = 81. So square root of 70 is between 8 and 9.
It is closer to 8 since 70 is closer to 64 than 81. Estimate ≈ 8.4.

Q3.question: Evaluate without a calculator: (a) 32 to the power (3/5)   (b) 81 to the power (-1/4)

steps: Part (b): Fourth root of 81 = 3. 81 to the power (-1/4) = 1/3.

Q4.question: Estimate the value of (square root of 47) x (cube root of 26), giving your answer to 1 significant figure.

steps: Square root of 47: 6 squared = 36, 7 squared = 49, so ≈ 6.9.
Cube root of 26: 2 cubed = 8, 3 cubed = 27, so ≈ 3.0.

Q5.question: Without a calculator, show that 27 to the power (4/3) + 16 to the power (3/4) = 89.

steps: 27 to the power (4/3): cube root of 27 = 3. Then 3 to the power 4 = 81.
16 to the power (3/4): fourth root of 16 = 2. Then 2 cubed = 8.
solution.final_answer: 27 to the power (4/3) + 16 to the power (3/4) = 81 + 8 = 89

summary: x to the power (m/n): take the nth root of x first, then raise to the power m.
x to the power -n = 1 / (x to the power n) — negative index means reciprocal.
To estimate a square root, find the two perfect squares either side of the number.
For estimation questions, round to 1 significant figure and calculate.
